title: Notice on potential impact of ”Arbitrary File Delete (Path Traversal) Vulnerability Leading to Code Execution" towards RICOH Streamline NX V3 PC Client
explanation: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the file deletion endpoint, allowing an attacker to delete arbitrary files on the C:\ drive as "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM". By exploiting this vulnerability, code execution as "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" can ultimately be achieved.
affect:
Any code executed inside the user's machine can have various impacts.

 

Confidentiality impact: The user's PC could be compromised if someone gains access to it. For example, an attacker could obtain the user's data (such as login data) or any confidential files if the malicious code is designed to share the data externally.

 

Integrity impact: Any code could be executed using the SLNX PC Client startup process, compromising data integrity. Existing files could be modified.

 

Availability impact: The product may not start again if the malicious code is designed to prevent it, requiring the SLNX PC Client to be reinstalled.
checkVersion:
Check the version of the product by one of the following methods on the PC running RICOH Streamline NX PC Client .
-Right-click the icon in the task tray and display the "Version Information" menu.
-Check the version of "RICOH Streamline NX PC Client" from the list of "Programs and Features" in the Control Panel.
measures:
Please update RICOH Streamline NX PC Client to a version that addresses this vulnerability.

 

Fixed version: 3.243.0
contact: Please contact your local Ricoh representative or dealer if you have any queries.
authenticityGuarantee: Please note that any copy or paraphrase of the text of this document that differs in content from the distribution URL link, or omits the URL, is an uncontrolled copy and may lack important information or contain factual errors.
